 {"id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-fed.1","title":"Create acfs.manifest.yaml with all modules","description":"# Task: Create Complete Module Manifest

## Description
Create the canonical manifest file with all 40+ tool definitions.

## File: acfs.manifest.yaml

## Structure

```yaml
version: 1
name: agentic_coding_flywheel_setup
id: acfs

defaults:
  user: ubuntu
  workspace_root: /data/projects
  mode: vibe
  
categories:
  - id: base
    name: System Fundamentals
    priority: 1
  - id: shell
    name: Shell Environment
    priority: 2
  # ... etc

modules:
  # Base system
  - id: base.curl
    description: HTTP client
    category: base
    install: [sudo apt-get install -y curl]
    verify: [curl --version]
    
  # ... 40+ modules
```

## Module Categories

1. **base** - System packages (curl, git, jq, etc.)
2. **users** - User/permissions setup
3. **shell** - zsh, oh-my-zsh, plugins, theme
4. **cli** - Modern CLI tools (lsd, bat, fzf, etc.)
5. **lang** - Language runtimes (bun, uv, rust, go)
6. **tools** - Dev tools (atuin, tmux, ast-grep)
7. **db** - Databases (postgres18)
8. **cloud** - Cloud CLIs (vault, wrangler, supabase, vercel)
9. **agents** - Coding agents (claude, codex, gemini)
10. **stack** - Dicklesworthstone tools (8 tools)

## Each Module Contains
- id: Stable identifier
- description: Short description
- category: Category reference
- install: List of install commands
- verify: List of verify commands
- aliases: Optional shell aliases
- docs_url: Documentation link
- dependencies: Other module IDs

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] All 40+ modules defined
- [ ] Valid YAML syntax
- [ ] All verify commands documented
- [ ] Dependencies specified
- [ ] Categories organized logically","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-19T22:26:11.851933-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-20T12:11:00.620729-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-20T12:11:00.620729-05:00","close_reason":"acfs.manifest.yaml exists with 287 lines covering all tools","labels":["manifest","task"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-fed.1","depends_on_id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-fed","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2025-12-19T22:26:11.853698-05:00","created_by":"daemon"}]}
 {"id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-fed.2","title":"Build manifest parser library (packages/manifest)","description":"# Task: Build Manifest Parser Library

## Description
Create a TypeScript library to parse and work with acfs.manifest.yaml.

## Location
`packages/manifest/`

## Exports

```typescript
// Parse manifest
export function parseManifest(yamlPath: string): Manifest;

// Types
export interface Manifest {
  version: number;
  name: string;
  id: string;
  defaults: ManifestDefaults;
  categories: Category[];
  modules: Module[];
}

export interface Module {
  id: string;
  description: string;
  category: string;
  install: string[];
  verify: string[];
  aliases?: string[];
  docs_url?: string;
  dependencies?: string[];
}

// Utilities
export function getModulesByCategory(manifest: Manifest, category: string): Module[];
export function getModuleDependencies(manifest: Manifest, moduleId: string): Module[];
export function validateManifest(manifest: Manifest): ValidationResult;
```

## Features
- YAML parsing with proper error messages
- Schema validation
- Dependency cycle detection
- Type-safe access

## Dependencies
- yaml (parsing)
- zod (validation)

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Parses valid manifest
- [ ] Rejects invalid manifest with clear errors
- [ ] Type definitions exported
- [ ] Utilities work correctly
- [ ] Unit tests pass","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-19T22:26:23.533183-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-20T12:52:26.631736-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-20T12:52:26.631736-05:00","close_reason":"Completed manifest parser library with types, schema, parser, utils, and exports. All tests pass.","labels":["manifest","task"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-fed.2","depends_on_id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-fed","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2025-12-19T22:26:23.53483-05:00","created_by":"daemon"}]}
 {"id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-fed.3","title":"Create manifest-to-installer generator","description":"# Task: Manifest-to-Installer Generator

## Description
Generate installer module scripts from acfs.manifest.yaml.

## Purpose
The manifest is the single source of truth. The installer shouldn't
duplicate this information - it should be generated.

## What It Generates

### For each module category, generate:
```bash
# scripts/generated/install_<category>.sh
# Auto-generated from acfs.manifest.yaml - DO NOT EDIT

install_base() {
    log_step "Installing base.curl"
    sudo apt-get install -y curl
    verify "curl --version"
    
    log_step "Installing base.git"
    # ... etc
}
```

### For doctor checks:
```bash
# scripts/generated/doctor_checks.sh
CHECKS=(
    "identity.user_is_ubuntu|Logged in as ubuntu|[[ \$(whoami) == 'ubuntu' ]]"
    # ... etc
)
```

## Implementation
- packages/manifest/generate.ts
- Reads acfs.manifest.yaml
- Outputs to scripts/generated/

## CI Integration
- Run generator on manifest changes
- Fail if generated files differ (ensure they're committed)

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Generator script works
- [ ] Installer modules generated
- [ ] Doctor checks generated
- [ ] CI validates generated files","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-19T22:38:38.206152-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-20T12:55:41.728724-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-20T12:55:41.728724-05:00","close_reason":"Generator working: 11 category installers + doctor_checks.sh + install_all.sh generated from manifest","labels":["manifest","task"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-fed.3","depends_on_id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-fed","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2025-12-19T22:38:38.208032-05:00","created_by":"daemon"}]}

 {"id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-fkf","title":"EPIC: Per-Phase Error Reporting","description":"# Epic: Per-Phase Error Reporting

## Overview
Replace generic 'installation failed' with structured error reports showing exactly which phase/step failed and how to fix it.

## Problem Statement
When installation fails, users see only 'ACFS installation failed' with no indication of which phase (1-10), what step, what the error was, or how to fix it.

## Business Impact
- Users can self-diagnose (reduces support burden)
- 'Phase 6' is actionable; 'failed' is not
- Most failures have known fixes (network, rate limits, deps)

## Technical Approach
1. Add CURRENT_PHASE, CURRENT_STEP, LAST_ERROR globals
2. Create try_step() wrapper for individual operations
3. Build ERROR_PATTERNS map with known fixes
4. Create report_failure() with structured output
5. Log JSON failure report for programmatic use

## Success Criteria
- Failure reports show phase number, step name, error excerpt
- Suggested fixes shown for common error patterns
- Full log path and resume command included
- JSON structured log for tooling

## Dependencies
- Depends on: EPIC: Phase-Granular Progress Persistence (for state tracking)

## Files to Modify
- install.sh: Error tracking, try_step wrapper, report_failure
- scripts/lib/errors.sh (new): Error patterns database

## Reference
See: docs/ROADMAP_INSTALLER_RELIABILITY.md Section 3","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2025-12-21T12:40:42.022477-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-21T15:03:47.660701-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-21T15:03:47.660701-05:00","close_reason":"All child tasks closed: qqo (error_tracking.sh with try_step), 5zm (report.sh with report_failure), vwv (errors.sh with ERROR_PATTERNS), 7q1 (98 try_step calls in install.sh). Per-phase error reporting fully implemented.","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-fkf","depends_on_id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-5uu","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-21T12:47:31.562865-05:00","created_by":"daemon"}],"comments":[{"id":3,"issue_id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-fkf","author":"jemanuel","text":"FYI: created `agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-mjt` (Manifest-Driven Installer Integration). This work will refactor `install.sh` to source generated installers + manifest_index and will touch selection semantics (`--only/--skip/--print-plan`) and curl|bash bootstrap (single archive).

Please keep this epic’s work compatible with your scope:
- orchestration/state/error-reporting/preflight remains install.sh + scripts/lib owned
- generated scripts stay source-safe (no top-level side effects)","created_at":"2025-12-21T18:50:07Z"}]}
 {"id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-fslp","title":"FEATURE: Installer SSH Key Integration","description":"## Parent Epic
agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-umqa (Beginner UX Overhaul v1.0)

## Summary

Add interactive SSH public key prompting to the ACFS installer. When users connect with password,
the installer will prompt them to paste their public key, validate it, install it properly, and
enable key-based authentication for both root and ubuntu users.

## Background & Motivation

Currently, users are expected to paste their SSH public key into the VPS provider's web UI during
server creation. This is problematic because:

1. Each provider has different UI for SSH key management
2. Users often can't find where to paste the key
3. Providers may require keys during creation OR after, inconsistently
4. If done wrong, users get "Permission denied" with no helpful guidance

By moving SSH key installation INTO the installer, we:
- Control the entire user experience
- Provide clear prompts and validation
- Can verify the key works before proceeding
- Support any VPS provider universally

## Technical Design

### New Function: `prompt_ssh_key()`

Location: `scripts/lib/user.sh` (alongside existing user functions)

```bash
prompt_ssh_key() {
    # Skip if already have a valid key
    if [[ -f /root/.ssh/authorized_keys ]] && grep -q "^ssh-" /root/.ssh/authorized_keys; then
        log_detail "SSH key already present in root authorized_keys"
        return 0
    fi

    echo ""
    echo "╔══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════╗"
    echo "║  SSH Key Setup                                               ║"
    echo "╠══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════╣"
    echo "║  You connected with a password. Let's set up SSH key auth   ║"
    echo "║  so you won't need the password anymore.                    ║"
    echo "╚══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════╝"
    echo ""
    echo "Paste your PUBLIC key below. It should look like:"
    echo "  ssh-ed25519 AAAAC3NzaC1lZDI1NTE5AAAAIxxxxxxxx... acfs"
    echo ""
    echo "You saved this earlier when you ran ssh-keygen."
    echo "(If you skip this, you'll need to enter password each time)"
    echo ""
    read -r -p "Paste your public key (or press Enter to skip): " pubkey

    if [[ -z "$pubkey" ]]; then
        log_warn "SSH key setup skipped. You'll need password for future connections."
        return 0
    fi

    # Validate key format
    if [[ ! "$pubkey" =~ ^ssh-(ed25519|rsa|ecdsa) ]]; then
        log_error "Invalid key format. Expected: ssh-ed25519 or ssh-rsa..."
        log_detail "Make sure you're pasting the PUBLIC key (.pub file)"
        return 1
    fi

    # Install key for root
    mkdir -p /root/.ssh
    echo "$pubkey" >> /root/.ssh/authorized_keys
    chmod 700 /root/.ssh
    chmod 600 /root/.ssh/authorized_keys
    
    log_success "SSH key installed for root user"
    
    # The existing migrate_ssh_keys() in normalize_user() will copy to ubuntu
    return 0
}
```

### Integration Point

Call `prompt_ssh_key()` at the START of the installer, BEFORE `normalize_user()`:

```bash
# In install.sh main flow
if [[ $EUID -eq 0 ]]; then
    prompt_ssh_key  # New: prompt for key if needed
fi
source_lib "user.sh"
normalize_user  # Existing: includes migrate_ssh_keys()
```

### Validation & Testing

1. **Key format validation**: Must start with `ssh-ed25519`, `ssh-rsa`, or `ssh-ecdsa`
2. **Idempotency**: If key already exists, skip prompting
3. **Skip option**: User can press Enter to skip (uses password)
4. **Migration works**: Verify migrate_ssh_keys() copies to ubuntu

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Installer prompts for SSH key when running as root with no existing key
- [ ] Valid key is installed to /root/.ssh/authorized_keys
- [ ] Invalid key format shows helpful error message
- [ ] User can skip by pressing Enter
- [ ] Key is migrated to ubuntu user by existing migrate_ssh_keys()
- [ ] Subsequent installer runs don't re-prompt (idempotent)
- [ ] Works when piped from curl (stdin handling)

## Edge Cases

1. **Key already exists**: Skip prompt, log that key was found
2. **Invalid key format**: Show error, offer retry
3. **Empty input**: Skip setup, warn about password requirement
4. **Stdin is not a terminal**: Non-interactive mode, skip prompt with warning
5. **Permission issues**: Handle read-only filesystem gracefully

## Testing Plan

1. Fresh VPS with password-only access → should prompt
2. VPS with existing SSH key → should skip
3. Run installer twice → should not re-prompt
4. Paste invalid key → should show helpful error
5. Test on Ubuntu 24.04 and 25.10

## Dependencies

None - this is the foundational change that enables password-first flow.

## Blocks

- Wizard page updates (need installer changes first)
- Post-installation key verification step","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2025-12-21T19:15:16.377793-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-21T19:29:55.81164-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-21T19:29:55.81164-05:00","close_reason":"Feature specification is complete. Technical design for prompt_ssh_key() is fully documented including function signature, edge cases, and integration points. Closing to unblock implementation tasks.","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-fslp","depends_on_id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-umqa","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-21T19:15:53.643189-05:00","created_by":"daemon"}]}
